blocks.content: And then, quietly, without looking at each other—
Cassandra's fingers closed around his, and he felt something release in his chest that he hadn't known he was holding.
Below them, the street moved on.
Life doing what life does—continuing carelessly in all directions.
On a forgotten bench, under a streetlight that barely reached—
two people sat in the particular, terrifying, irreversible quiet of choosing each other.
Ethan looked down at their hands.
He had spent a long time making sure nothing got to matter too much.
He had been very good at it.
He thought about what Nathan had said.
She sounds like the kind of person you meet once.
Cassandra's thumb moved slightly against his, the smallest possible motion, not a word but something better.
Barely above the sound of the street.
The city went quiet around them the way cities do when something is happening that has nothing to do with cities.
And for the first time in a long time—
Ethan didn't want to be careful.
For as long as it was his to hold.
